title: Reproduce the Vibrio nigripulchritudo multi-record circular genome in the web app
summary: Build a circular multi-record canvas for two chromosomes and four plasmids from a single RefSeq GBFF file, using automatic record sizing, explicit row placement, GC content, GC skew, and a left legend.
estimatedTime: 5-15 min after the input is available

downloads.label: GCF_015097735.1
downloads.note: Download the RefSeq genomic GBFF and use it as the GenBank input.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and choose Circular before adding the GenBank file.

media.alt: Circular mode selected in the web app header.
media.caption: Select Circular.

manualSteps.title: Upload the GenBank file
manualSteps.body: Upload GCF_015097735.1_ASM1509773v1_genomic.gbff. The app will detect the two chromosomes and four plasmids in the file.

media.alt: Circular input panel with the Vibrio GBFF file selected.
media.caption: GCF_015097735.1_ASM1509773v1_genomic.gbff is selected as the GenBank file.

manualSteps.title: Set the circular layout
manualSteps.body: Set Output Prefix to Vnig_TUMSAT-TG-2018, Track type to tuckin, Palette to orchid, Separate strands to on, Legend to left, and keep CDS, rRNA, tRNA, tmRNA, ncRNA, and repeat_region selected.

operations.title: Palette selector
operations.body: Open the default color palette selector and choose orchid.

media.alt: Colors card with the default color palette menu open and the orchid option highlighted.
media.caption: Open the default color palette selector in the Colors card and choose the highlighted orchid option.

operations.title: Legend position selector
operations.body: Open Legend Position and choose Left.

media.alt: Title and Legend card with the Legend Position menu open and the Left option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Legend Position in the Title & Legend card and choose the highlighted Left option.

manualSteps.title: Enable Multi-record canvas
manualSteps.body: Turn on Multi-record canvas. Use auto size mode, minimum radius ratio 0.55, column gap 0.1, row gap 0.05, and place records #1 and #2 in row 1 and #3 through #6 in row 2.
manualSteps.note: The two large chromosomes sit in the top row. The four smaller plasmids share the second row.

operations.title: Multi-record canvas controls
operations.body: Set the multi-record canvas controls shown in the screenshot.

media.alt: Multi-record canvas controls with auto sizing, minimum radius ratio, gaps, and record order rows.
media.caption: Set Multi-record canvas, automatic sizing, gaps, and record rows.

actions.script: () => new Promise((resolve, reject) => { const expected = '#1@1,#2@1,#3@2,#4@2,#5@2,#6@2'; const deadline = Date.now() + 30000; const check = () => { const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; const actual = (Array.isArray(app?.adv?.multi_record_positions) ? app.adv.multi_record_positions : []).map((entry) => `${entry.selector}@${entry.row}`).join(','); if (actual === expected) { resolve(); return; } if (Date.now() > deadline) { reject(new Error(`Expected Vnig multi-record positions ${expected}, got ${actual}`)); return; } setTimeout(check, 100); }; check(); })

manualSteps.title: Add Title
manualSteps.body: Set Plot Title to Vibrio nigripulchritudo TUMSAT-TG-2018, complete genome. For this figure, choose Bottom in Plot Title Position.

operations.title: Plot Title Position
operations.body: Open Plot Title Position and choose Bottom.

media.alt: Title and Legend card with the Plot Title Position menu open and the Bottom option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Plot Title Position in the Title & Legend card and choose the highlighted Bottom option.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The preview should show chromosome 1 and chromosome 2 above plasmids pVNTG1 through pVNTG4, with GC content and GC skew rings inside each record.

operations.title: Generated multi-record preview
operations.body: Inspect the generated preview with two chromosomes and four plasmids.

media.alt: Actual Vibrio multi-record tutorial UI showing the generated multi-record preview with two chromosomes and four plasmids.
media.caption: The generated preview uses automatic radius scaling for two chromosomes and four plasmids.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a CDS feature
manualSteps.body: Click a CDS feature in the interactive SVG to inspect its label, record ID, location, protein_id, qualifiers, and sequence.

operations.title: CDS feature popup
operations.body: Click a CDS feature so the CDS feature popup is visible.

media.alt: Zoomed Vibrio multi-record crop with the clicked CDS on chromosome 1 and its feature popup.
media.caption: The zoomed crop keeps the clicked chromosome CDS and popup metadata readable.

troubleshooting.title: Only one record is shown
troubleshooting.body: Check that Multi-record canvas is enabled. Without that option, circular mode can render records separately instead of placing them in one canvas.

troubleshooting.title: The plasmids are too small
troubleshooting.body: Raise the minimum radius ratio, or switch size mode if you need less proportional scaling between chromosomes and plasmids.

troubleshooting.title: The rows differ from the Gallery figure
troubleshooting.body: Check record positions #1@1, #2@1, #3@2, #4@2, #5@2, and #6@2, plus column gap ratio 0.1 and row gap ratio 0.05.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Check the GBFF input, tuckin track type, orchid palette, separate strands, left legend, bottom title, multi-record size mode auto, and minimum radius ratio 0.55.
